concept_name: Same Lap Every Time — T = 2πm/qB Is Independent of Speed
source_book: DC Pandey Class 12 Ch.4 Moving Charges and Magnetism — table-of-contents reference only (the cyclotron / period-of-circular-motion heading, scope only); teaching authored from first principles. NCERT consulted for the Indian real-world anchor only (the VECC cyclotron). This is the PERIOD / TIMING sibling of circular_motion_charge_in_uniform_B: it surfaces the one quantity #4 deliberately hid — the time for one full orbit, T = 2πm/qB — and proves T is INDEPENDENT of speed v and radius r. A faster charge traces a BIGGER circle but completes each lap in exactly the SAME time, because the extra distance is paid for by the extra speed (they cancel). The single surfaced quantity is T, and only as a RELATIVE lap-timer (a ring-fill that freezes on one revolution and relabels itself T) — never a seconds value, never a wall-clock, never a frequency number. It deliberately does NOT re-derive r = mv/qB (it CITES #4's result), does NOT show the force magnitude F = qvB sin(theta) (that belongs to magnetic_force_moving_charge — F here is a direction-only fixed glyph, Rule 29), and does NOT touch Ampere / current-loop / dipole / solenoid content. Conceptual-only ship (no mode_overrides). EPIC-L-first (no epic_c_branches; misconceptions confronted inside EPIC-L via Rule 16a misconception_watch + straightforward contrast beats). Renders on field_3d via the cyclotron_period scenario, which INVERTS radius_in_uniform_field: instead of holding arc-speed constant to SUPPRESS the period, it holds the PERIOD constant (a single shared omega = 2π/T_visual drives every charge) and lets the radii differ, so two charges of different speed/size tie by construction and re-converge at the start every lap. Lighter atom by founder carve-out: no assessment block, no coverage_map. Retrofitted from the Socratic model to the straightforward Rule 31 model (2026-07-04): guided distinct-motion beats (no predict-then-reveal prediction pauses, no wait_for_answer, no pause_after_ms; narration drives on-canvas glow, motion runs on the state clock), each state exposing only its own contextual control (per-state visible_controls — S1/S2/S3 zero-control watch beats with auto-choreography (single-orbit timer freeze, dual-orbit race, equation cancel), S4 explore exposes m/v/q/B). The cyclotron_period per-state contextual-control panel was added the same gated way as the circular_loop cl_ pattern: cyc_*_row wrappers + applyVisibleControls + cycInteracted trusted-grab seize + on-entry seed from variable_overrides + a STATE_4 idle v-sweep (cyc.idle_v_sweep) that auto-demonstrates size-grows / clock-holds until the teacher grabs a slider.

real_world_anchor.primary: At the VECC cyclotron in Kolkata, charged particles are whipped round and round through a uniform magnetic field, and a voltage kicks them faster twice every loop. As they speed up they swing out onto a bigger and bigger circle — yet EVERY lap takes exactly the same time. That is the whole trick: because the lap time T = 2πm/qB does not depend on speed, the machine can kick on one steady, fixed rhythm and stay perfectly in step with the particle no matter how fast it gets. It is the same VECC machine you met when learning that a magnetic force only steers and that the circle's size is r = mv/qB — now look at the CLOCK: the size keeps growing, but the lap time never changes.

m.name: Mass of the moving charge (numerator of T = 2πm/qB; bigger m = longer period AND wider circle). Visual-scaling knob, the number is never shown on the canvas.

v.name: Speed of the moving charge (sets circle SIZE via r = mv/qB but CANCELS out of T = 2πm/qB; faster = bigger circle, SAME period). Visual-scaling knob, the number is never shown on the canvas.

q_mag.name: Magnitude of the charge (denominator of T = 2πm/qB; more charge = shorter period). Visual-scaling knob, the number is never shown on the canvas.

B.name: Magnetic field strength (denominator of T = 2πm/qB; stronger field = shorter period). Visual-scaling knob, the number is never shown on the canvas.

formulas.period_from_geometry: One lap is the distance round divided by the speed: T = 2πr/v
formulas.radius_from_last_lesson: The radius is r = mv/qB, cited from the previous concept and NOT re-derived here
formulas.period_substituted: Put r = mv/qB into T = 2πr/v: T = 2π(mv/qB)/v
formulas.period_v_cancels: The v on top meets the v on the bottom and they cancel exactly
formulas.period: T = 2πm/qB — the period depends ONLY on m, q and B, never on v or r
formulas.frequency: As a frequency, f = 1/T = qB/2πm (one aside line)

constraints: Teaches ONLY the period T = 2πm/qB and its independence from speed v and radius r. Slider INPUT values for m, v, q, B are allowed; no other number reaches the canvas.

The period readout is RELATIVE (a lap-timer that ticks, freezes on one full revolution, and relabels itself T) — never a seconds value, never a wall-clock, never a frequency number. Every state flags show_period_readout: true (the INVERSE of #4's hide_period_readout: true).
NO force magnitude — no F = qvB, no qvB sin(theta), no Newton number. Every state flags hide_magnitude_readout: true; the F arrow is a direction-only fixed glyph pointing to the centre (Rule 29).
Radius r is NOT re-derived — it appears ONLY as a cited symbol in the STATE_3 substitution; no metres value, no radius readout bar.
NO Ampere, NO current loop, NO magnetic dipole, NO solenoid.
Speed magnitude is NOT encoded in the v-glyph length (the arrow is a fixed length). The fast charge's higher speed is shown ONLY by its bigger circle and the race timing.
The period is independent of speed and radius: T = 2πm/qB has no v and no r. A faster charge traces a bigger circle but takes the same lap time.
Both charges share omega = 2π/T_visual, so they have the SAME period by construction and re-converge at the start every lap (the tie is geometry, not a coincidence).
f = 1/T = qB/2πm: one aside line in STATE_3, no separate frequency readout.

aha_moment.statement: A faster charge runs a bigger circle in the same time — period never changes.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: A slow charge and a fast charge are released together from one start line; the fast one swings onto a visibly bigger circle, yet both return to the start the very same instant and both lap-timers freeze on an identical T.

scene_composition.text: a charge loops round and round on one steady circle in a magnetic field

watch the clock in
the corner — it ticks
as the charge orbits
one full lap takes a definite time — we call it the period, T

tts_sentences.text_en: A charge loops round and round on one steady circle in a magnetic field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक charge एक steady circle पर बार-बार घूमता है, एक magnetic field में।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక charge ఒక steady circle పై మళ్ళీ మళ్ళీ తిరుగుతూ ఉంటుంది, ఒక magnetic field లో।

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the clock in the corner tick through one full lap, then freeze.
tts_sentences.text_hi: कोने में clock को देखो — यह एक पूरे lap तक tick करता है, फिर रुक जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: మూలలో clock ను చూడండి — అది ఒక పూర్తి lap వరకు tick అవుతుంది, తర్వాత ఆగిపోతుంది।

tts_sentences.text_en: That one lap time is the period T — and the big question is whether it depends on speed.
tts_sentences.text_hi: वह एक lap का time है period T — और बड़ा सवाल यह है कि क्या यह speed पर depend करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఆ ఒక lap సమయమే period T — మరియు పెద్ద ప్రశ్న ఏమిటంటే అది speed పై depend అవుతుందా అని।

STATE_2.title: Faster, but Same Lap — the Race

scene_composition.text: released together -> a tie: same period T, whatever the speed

the fast charge swings
onto a BIGGER circle —
the slow one stays
tight
a tie — both back at the start the same instant, same T

tts_sentences.text_en: Now two charges from the same start line — I speed the second one up, so it swings onto a bigger circle.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब एक ही start line से दो charges — मैं दूसरे को speed up करता हूँ, तो यह एक bigger circle पर निकल जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు ఒకే start line నుండి రెండు charges — నేను రెండవదాన్ని speed up చేస్తాను, కాబట్టి అది ఒక bigger circle పైకి వెళ్తుంది।

tts_sentences.text_en: Released together, they both return to the start at the very same instant — both timers freeze on the same period T.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक साथ छोड़े गए, दोनों बिल्कुल एक ही instant में start पर वापस आते हैं — दोनों timers एक ही period T पर रुक जाते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: కలిసి వదిలిపెట్టబడ్డాయి, రెండూ సరిగ్గా ఒకే instant లో start వద్దకు తిరిగొస్తాయి — రెండు timers ఒకే period T పై ఆగిపోతాయి।

tts_sentences.text_en: It is a tie: the faster charge runs a bigger lap, but its extra speed pays for the extra distance exactly.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह एक tie है: faster charge एक bigger lap दौड़ता है, लेकिन उसकी extra speed extra distance की कीमत बिल्कुल चुका देती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది ఒక tie: faster charge ఒక bigger lap పరిగెత్తుతుంది, కానీ దాని extra speed extra distance ని సరిగ్గా చెల్లిస్తుంది।

misconception_watch.belief: A faster charge takes LESS time per loop.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The slow and fast charges are released together and both return to the start the same instant, both timers freezing on an identical T.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Faster means a bigger circle too — the two effects cancel, so the lap time is unchanged.

misconception_watch.belief: A bigger circle takes LONGER to go round.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The fast charge owns the visibly bigger circle yet ties the small one exactly.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: The bigger circle is run at higher speed, so it still takes T = 2πm/qB.

scene_composition.text: one lap is the distance round divided by the speed: T = 2πr/v

put in r = mv/qB —
now a v on top meets
a v on the bottom,
and they cancel
T = 2πm/qB — no v, no r; the period rests on m, q and B alone

tts_sentences.text_en: One lap is the distance round divided by the speed: period T equals 2-pi-r over speed v.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक lap है distance round को speed से भाग देना: period T equals 2-pi-r over speed v।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక lap అంటే distance round ని speed తో భాగించడం: period T equals 2-pi-r over speed v।

tts_sentences.text_en: Substitute the radius r equals m v over q B, and a speed v appears on top and on the bottom — they cancel exactly.
tts_sentences.text_hi: radius r equals m v over q B substitute करो, और ऊपर तथा नीचे एक speed v आ जाती है — वे बिल्कुल cancel हो जाती हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: radius r equals m v over q B substitute చేయండి, మరియు పైన మరియు కింద ఒక speed v వస్తుంది — అవి సరిగ్గా cancel అవుతాయి।

tts_sentences.text_en: So period T equals 2-pi-m over q B, with no speed v and no radius r — it rests on mass m, charge q and magnetic field B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तो period T equals 2-pi-m over q B, कोई speed v नहीं और कोई radius r नहीं — यह mass m, charge q और magnetic field B पर टिका है।
tts_sentences.text_te: కాబట్టి period T equals 2-pi-m over q B, speed v లేదు మరియు radius r లేదు — ఇది mass m, charge q మరియు magnetic field B పై ఆధారపడుతుంది।

STATE_4.title: Your Turn — Change the Speed, Watch the Clock

scene_composition.text: four sliders — m, v, q, B — one charge, and a live lap-timer

scene_composition.label: v (speed) — resizes the circle, holds the clock
m, q, B — these move the clock

scene_composition.text:
the live lap-timer
holds steady on v,
but moves on m, q
or B
v is nowhere in T = 2πm/qB — speed and size never change the period

tts_sentences.text_en: Your turn — mass m, speed v, charge q, magnetic field B, one charge and a live lap-timer.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब तुम्हारी बारी — mass m, speed v, charge q, magnetic field B, एक charge और एक live lap-timer।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీ వంతు — mass m, speed v, charge q, magnetic field B, ఒక charge మరియు ఒక live lap-timer।

tts_sentences.text_en: Speed v up and the circle grows, but the lap-time holds dead steady — speed v is nowhere in T equals 2-pi-m over q B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: speed v बढ़ाओ और circle बड़ा होता है, लेकिन lap-time बिल्कुल steady रहता है — speed v, T equals 2-pi-m over q B में कहीं नहीं है।
tts_sentences.text_te: speed v పెంచండి, circle పెద్దదవుతుంది, కానీ lap-time పూర్తిగా steady గా ఉంటుంది — speed v అనేది T equals 2-pi-m over q B లో ఎక్కడా లేదు।

tts_sentences.text_en: Change mass m or magnetic field B and the clock moves — those are the only things the period T depends on.
tts_sentences.text_hi: mass m या magnetic field B बदलो और clock move करता है — period T केवल इन्हीं पर depend करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: mass m లేదా magnetic field B మార్చండి, clock move అవుతుంది — period T కేవలం వీటిపై మాత్రమే depend అవుతుంది।

tts_sentences.text_en: A charge loops round and round on one steady circle in a magnetic field.
Watch the clock in the corner tick through one full lap, then freeze.
That one lap time is the period T — and the big question is whether it depends on speed.

STATE_2.label: Faster, but same lap — the race
STATE_2.caption: Bigger circle, same T

tts_sentences.text_en: Now two charges from the same start line — I speed the second one up, so it swings onto a bigger circle.
Released together, they both return to the start at the very same instant — both timers freeze on the same T.
It is a tie: the faster charge runs a bigger lap, but its extra speed pays for the extra distance exactly.
One lap is the distance round divided by the speed: period T equals 2-pi-r over speed v.
Substitute the radius r equals m v over q B, and a speed v appears on top and on the bottom — they cancel exactly.
So period T equals 2-pi-m over q B, with no speed v and no radius r — it rests on mass m, charge q and magnetic field B.

STATE_4.label: Your turn — change the speed, watch the clock
STATE_4.caption: T holds steady on v

tts_sentences.text_en: Your turn — mass m, speed v, charge q, magnetic field B, one charge and a live lap-timer.
Speed v up and the circle grows, but the lap-time holds dead steady — speed v is nowhere in T equals 2-pi-m over q B.
Change mass m or magnetic field B and the clock moves — those are the only things the period T depends on.
